commits.message: feat(context): implement persistent context management for llm orchestrator

wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the LLM_analyzer_context project. Explain its purpose as an autonomous AI-driven system for vibration and industrial signal analysis using LLM orchestration and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). Describe the high-level architecture, including the roles of the LLMOrchestrator, GUI interface, tool modules, and context management. Highlight key features such as autonomous pipeline generation, multimodal evaluation, and integration with domain-specific knowledge. Provide a conceptual overview accessible to beginners while including technical depth for developers, such as the use of Google Gemini API, LangChain, and ChromaDB. Illustrate the system workflow from user input to final analysis with a diagram. Include real-world use cases like bearing fault detection and gearbox analysis. Address prerequisites for understanding the system, including signal processing and LLM fundamentals.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Orchestration Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the LLMOrchestrator component. Describe its role as the central decision-making hub that manages the autonomous analysis pipeline. Explain how it generates action sequences based on user objectives and metaknowledge, coordinates tool execution, and handles iterative refinement through feedback loops. Detail the state machine design that tracks pipeline progression, including initialization, execution, evaluation, and termination states. Document the integration points with ContextManager for state persistence, prompt_assembler for dynamic prompt generation, rag_builder for domain knowledge retrieval, and quantitative_parameterization_module for result assessment. Illustrate the control flow using sequence diagrams showing interactions during pipeline execution. Discuss design trade-offs such as synchronous vs asynchronous processing, error recovery strategies, and handling partial failures. Include performance considerations for minimizing LLM call latency and optimizing pipeline efficiency.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management and Persistence
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the state management system in the LLMOrchestrator. Explain how execution state is persisted across sessions using pickle serialization and directory-based run storage under ./run_state/{run_id}. Document the structure of saved artifacts including metaknowledge.json, temporary data files, and result pickles. Describe the role of ContextManager in maintaining conversation history and metadata across LLM interactions. Detail how state restoration works upon resuming analysis and the integration with the GUI via log_queue for real-time updates. Include examples of state corruption scenarios and recovery strategies. Address best practices for managing disk space and securing sensitive run data.

wiki_catalogs.name: RAG Index Construction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the RAG index construction process. Explain how rag_builder.py ingests tool documentation and domain-specific knowledge from TOOLS_REFERENCE.md and other sources to build a searchable ChromaDB vector store. Detail the document loading, text splitting, and embedding generation pipeline using LangChain components. Describe configuration options for embedding models and chunking strategies. Provide step-by-step instructions for adding new tool documentation or domain knowledge to the index. Include code examples showing how the index is initialized and persisted. Address common issues such as incomplete indexing, embedding mismatches, and performance bottlenecks during large-scale index creation. Offer optimization tips for balancing retrieval accuracy and indexing speed.

wiki_catalogs.name: Signal Processing Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for signal processing filter tools: bandpass_filter, highpass_filter, and lowpass_filter. For each tool, explain its purpose in vibration and signal analysis, particularly in isolating frequency bands relevant to industrial fault detection. Document function signatures, parameters (e.g., cutoff frequencies, filter order), return values, and internal implementation using scipy.signal. Include practical usage examples from the codebase showing how these filters are applied to time-series data. Explain the underlying digital filter design (e.g., Butterworth, Chebyshev) if applicable. Link to corresponding markdown documentation for configuration best practices. Address common issues such as phase distortion, filter instability, and edge effects, with mitigation strategies. Provide performance tips for real-time or large dataset processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Metaknowledge Prompt Construction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the metaknowledge prompt construction system. Explain how metaknowledge_prompt.txt and its v2 variant are used to extract structural and contextual information from input data, including signal characteristics, metadata, and domain-specific features. Describe the role of these prompts in initializing the analysis pipeline by generating structured JSON metaknowledge. Detail the template variables such as {data_shape}, {sampling_rate}, and {user_objective}, and how they are populated during runtime. Provide concrete examples of input prompts and resulting metaknowledge outputs from actual execution traces. Document the evolution from v1 to v2 in terms of clarity, specificity, and error reduction. Include best practices for extending templates with new data modalities. Address common issues like incomplete extraction, type mismatches, and ambiguous user objectives. Discuss how prompt design impacts the accuracy and completeness of downstream pipeline decisions.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for API configuration in mcpsettings.json. Explain each field related to LLM API settings, including model selection, API keys, base URLs, and request timeout configurations. Document how these settings affect orchestration behavior and response quality. Provide examples of valid configurations for Google Gemini and potential fallback models. Include security recommendations for managing API keys, such as environment variable integration and key rotation. Demonstrate how to adjust temperature, max tokens, and other generation parameters for different analysis objectives. Address common configuration errors and their troubleshooting steps, such as authentication failures or rate limiting.

wiki_catalogs.name: Bearing Fault Detection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop a comprehensive guide on using the autonomous analysis system for bearing fault detection. Focus on the envelope spectrum analysis workflow implemented in create_envelope_spectrum.py. Explain the physics of bearing faults, characteristic fault frequencies, and how envelope demodulation reveals hidden periodic impacts in vibration signals. Walk through a complete example: loading bearing vibration data, specifying a fault detection objective, and interpreting the automatically generated pipeline that includes bandpass filtering and envelope spectrum transformation. Use test_run_button.py to illustrate test execution flow and baseline_measurement.py for performance comparison. Include configuration parameters for sensitivity tuning, visualization of diagnostic features (e.g., BPFO, BPFI harmonics), and interpretation guidelines. Highlight how the LLMOrchestrator selects this method based on metaknowledge about rotational machinery and expected fault signatures. Provide real-world data examples and accuracy benchmarks from baseline_results.json.

wiki_catalogs.name: Bandpass Filter
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the bandpass_filter tool. Explain its role in isolating specific frequency bands in vibration signals, particularly for detecting bearing faults or gear mesh frequencies in industrial machinery. Document the function signature, parameters (including lowcut, highcut, fs, and order), return values, and internal implementation using scipy.signal.butter and scipy.signal.filtfilt. Provide code examples from the codebase showing how it is applied within analysis pipelines. Describe the use of zero-phase filtering via filtfilt to prevent time-domain distortion. Link to bandpass_filter.md for configuration best practices. Address common issues such as filter roll-off, transition band selection, and instability at extreme cutoff ratios, with practical mitigation strategies. Include performance considerations for batch processing and real-time applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: FFT Spectrum
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the FFT Spectrum transform tool. Explain its purpose in converting time-domain vibration signals into frequency-domain representations to identify dominant frequencies and harmonic content. Document the implementation using numpy.fft, including preprocessing steps like detrending and windowing (e.g., Hanning). Describe input signal requirements, parameter configuration (nfft, sampling rate), and output structure (frequency bins, magnitude spectrum). Provide code examples showing integration within analysis pipelines for fault detection in rotating machinery. Explain how spectral peaks correlate with mechanical issues such as imbalance or misalignment. Include performance considerations for large datasets and guidance on interpreting results in industrial diagnostics. Link to best practices in the accompanying markdown guide for optimal resolution and leakage reduction.

wiki_catalogs.name: NMF Decomposition
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Non-negative Matrix Factorization (NMF) decomposition tool used in vibration signal analysis. Explain the mathematical foundation of NMF and its application in isolating fault-related components from composite vibration signals. Document the implementation in decompose_matrix_nmf.py, including function signatures, parameters (e.g., number of components, convergence tolerance, initialization method), and return values. Describe how the algorithm processes time-series data into basis and coefficient matrices, enabling physical interpretation of machine health states. Provide code examples showing integration with the LLMOrchestrator and usage within automated pipelines. Illustrate input/output data formats and compatibility with downstream tools like select_component.py. Address performance considerations for large datasets, convergence diagnostics, and tuning strategies for industrial use cases. Include common pitfalls such as overfitting, non-unique solutions, and initialization sensitivity, with mitigation techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Persistent Context Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the persistent context management system. Document its purpose, implementation details, API interfaces, and integration patterns. Include practical examples and troubleshooting guidance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Context-Aware Prompt Engineering
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for context-aware prompt engineering. Document the architecture of contextual prompt assembly, implementation details of context injection, API interfaces for context-aware templates, and integration patterns with the ContextManager. Include practical examples of contextual prompts and troubleshooting guidance for common context-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Getting Started guide. Provide step-by-step installation instructions for setting up the Python environment, installing dependencies via requirements.txt, and configuring API credentials in mcpsettings.json. Include prerequisites such as Python version, required libraries (numpy, scipy, matplotlib, h5py, CustomTkinter), and Google Gemini API access. Walk through launching the application via app.py and navigating the initial GUI interface. Provide a quick start tutorial: loading sample data, specifying an analysis objective, and initiating the autonomous pipeline. Include troubleshooting tips for common setup issues like missing dependencies or authentication errors. Make the guide beginner-friendly with screenshots and code snippets where applicable.

wiki_catalogs.name: Context Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the ContextManager component. Explain its implementation of persistent state management across analysis sessions using pickle serialization. Document the data model for storing conversation history, intermediate results, execution state, and configuration parameters. Describe the API methods for saving, loading, and restoring context, including safeguards against corruption and version incompatibility. Illustrate how context persistence enables resumable pipeline execution and supports iterative refinement of analysis objectives. Explain the integration with LLMOrchestrator to maintain continuity between user interactions and background processing. Address security considerations for storing sensitive data and credentials in serialized context files. Provide examples of context manipulation for debugging and result reproduction. Include best practices for managing context size and optimizing load/save performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Execution Loop and Pipeline Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the main execution loop within the run_analysis_pipeline method. Explain the iterative process from initial data loading through dynamic tool selection, script generation, subprocess execution, and evaluation feedback. Cover the safety mechanisms such as max_iterations limit and infinite loop prevention. Detail how the orchestrator handles both successful and failed evaluations, including action rollback when results are deemed not useful. Illustrate the flow of control between _fetch_next_action, _execute_current_pipeline, and _evaluate_result. Provide concrete examples of loop behavior during bearing fault detection workflows. Address debugging techniques for stalled or divergent pipelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Context Retrieval Mechanism
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the context retrieval mechanism in the RAG system. Explain how rag_builder.py performs similarity searches against the ChromaDB vector store based on user objectives and data characteristics. Describe the query formulation process, relevance scoring, and top-k result selection. Detail how retrieved context fragments are injected into the metaknowledge_prompt.txt template via prompt_assembler.py to enhance LLM reasoning. Illustrate with real examples how retrieved tool documentation improves decision accuracy in pipeline construction. Document parameters controlling retrieval behavior such as similarity thresholds and result limits. Include troubleshooting guidance for low-relevance retrievals and strategies for improving query-context alignment. Discuss latency considerations and caching opportunities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Transform Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for spectral and time-frequency transform tools: FFT spectrum, envelope spectrum, spectrogram, and CSC (Cepstral Spectral Correlation) map. For each transform, describe its analytical purpose in detecting periodic impulses, modulation effects, or non-stationary behavior in vibration signals. Document input requirements, algorithmic implementation (e.g., FFT, Hilbert transform, short-time Fourier transform), output formats, and visualization integration. Include code examples showing how these transforms are used in diagnostic workflows. Explain mathematical foundations and domain-specific interpretation (e.g., envelope spectrum for bearing fault frequencies). Address parameter selection (window size, overlap, detrending) and computational performance. Link to markdown guides for optimal configuration in different industrial scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline Design Prompts
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the pipeline design prompt system. Explain how meta_template_prompt.txt and its updated v2 version guide the LLM in constructing executable analysis pipelines based on retrieved context and metaknowledge. Describe the structure of the prompt, including sections for tool registry, constraints, and execution guidelines. Detail how dynamic elements like {available_tools}, {current_context}, and {analysis_history} are injected to maintain coherence across iterations. Provide examples of rendered prompts that lead to valid action sequences involving signal processing, transforms, and decomposition tools. Discuss the role of few-shot examples embedded in the template to improve structural consistency. Document differences between v1 and v2, focusing on improved instruction clarity and reduced hallucination. Include best practices for adding new tools to the registry and ensuring prompt compatibility. Address common failure modes such as invalid tool chaining and infinite loops, and how prompt design mitigates them.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Mechanisms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the authentication system implemented in authentication.py. Explain how API credentials are securely loaded, validated, and passed to the LLMOrchestrator. Document the authentication workflow, including token validation, credential caching, and error handling for expired or invalid keys. Describe integration points with mcpsettings.json and runtime security checks. Provide code examples showing secure credential access patterns. Address potential vulnerabilities and mitigation strategies, such as preventing credential leakage in logs. Include guidance on extending the system for multi-user or enterprise authentication scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Gearbox Fault Identification
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for gearbox fault identification using Non-Negative Matrix Factorization (NMF) decomposition. Explain how decompose_matrix_nmf.py separates mixed vibration sources in multi-stage gearboxes by exploiting amplitude modulation structures. Describe the use case where overlapping frequency components from different gears make traditional FFT analysis insufficient. Provide a step-by-step example: loading multi-channel gearbox data, defining a fault isolation objective, and analyzing the autonomous pipeline that applies NMF followed by component selection. Detail how select_component.py identifies fault-relevant components based on sparsity and periodicity metrics. Use test_run_button.py to demonstrate execution and result inspection. Include guidance on interpreting factorized components, validating results against known fault patterns, and tuning NMF parameters (number of components, sparsity constraints). Explain how the RAG system informs the LLMOrchestrator about gearbox kinematics to guide decomposition strategy.

wiki_catalogs.name: Highpass Filter
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the highpass_filter tool. Explain its purpose in removing low-frequency noise or drift from vibration signals, such as eliminating rotational speed harmonics or sensor drift. Document the function signature, parameters (cutoff frequency, fs, order), return values, and implementation using scipy.signal.butter and scipy.signal.filtfilt. Provide usage examples from the codebase demonstrating preprocessing steps in fault detection workflows. Explain the rationale behind zero-phase filtering and its importance in preserving transient features. Reference highpass_filter.md for recommended settings. Address common pitfalls like attenuation of near-cutoff frequencies and ringing artifacts, with guidance on optimal filter order and transition width. Include performance tips for large-scale signal processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Envelope Spectrum
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Envelope Spectrum tool, designed to detect early-stage bearing faults by analyzing amplitude modulation in vibration signals. Detail the two-stage process: bandpass filtering around resonant frequencies followed by Hilbert transform demodulation and FFT. Explain the role of each step in extracting fault-related impulses masked by noise. Document parameters such as center frequency, bandwidth, and filtering order. Provide usage examples in diagnosing inner/outer race defects, with reference to characteristic fault frequencies. Include visualization outputs and integration with diagnostic decision logic. Address common pitfalls like improper filter selection and strategies for optimizing detection sensitivity. Reference the markdown guide for recommended settings across different machine types and operating conditions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Selection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the component selection module that evaluates and filters NMF-derived components based on diagnostic relevance. Detail the logic in select_component.py for scoring components using metrics such as sparsity, periodicity, kurtosis, and frequency band alignment with known fault signatures. Explain how this tool interfaces with the quantitative_parameterization_module.py to inform decision-making in the LLMOrchestrator. Document input requirements (decomposed matrices, metadata) and output (selected component indices, quality scores). Provide usage examples within autonomous analysis workflows where the LLM chooses components for envelope analysis or visualization. Include configuration options for thresholding and scoring weights. Address challenges like false positives in noisy environments and ambiguity in multi-fault scenarios, with recommendations for parameter tuning and validation against ground truth data.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Interface Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the User Interface components of the application. Describe the main window layout, including data loading panel, objective input field, visualization canvas, flowchart navigator, and control buttons. Explain how user inputs are processed by the ContextManager and passed to the LLMOrchestrator. Document interaction patterns such as drag-and-drop data loading, real-time flowchart updates, and result visualization. Include usage examples for common workflows: starting a new analysis, reviewing intermediate results, and modifying objectives mid-pipeline. Address accessibility considerations and responsive behavior. Provide guidance on interpreting visual outputs and navigating the execution history. Link UI actions to backend processes for deeper understanding.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dynamic Prompt Assembly
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the prompt_assembler module. Detail how it constructs context-aware prompts by combining user objectives, data characteristics, and domain knowledge retrieved from the RAG system. Explain the template system using files like meta_template_prompt.txt and metaknowledge_prompt.txt, including versioning between v1 and v2 templates. Document the assembly process for different prompt types: metaknowledge construction, tool selection, pipeline evaluation, and result interpretation. Describe the parameterization system that injects dynamic values such as signal statistics, file metadata, and previous results into prompts. Illustrate the integration with LLMOrchestrator for generating targeted queries and with rag_builder for enriching prompts with domain-specific knowledge. Provide examples of assembled prompts from actual execution traces. Address common issues like prompt overflow, template errors, and context leakage, along with mitigation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dynamic Script Generation and Execution
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the _translate_actions_to_code and _execute_current_pipeline methods. Explain how the orchestrator converts a sequence of Action objects into executable Python scripts using deterministic code generation. Document the import resolution strategy via tool_submodule_map and dynamic sys.path modification for module access. Describe the subprocess execution model with timeout enforcement and error handling for CalledProcessError and TimeoutExpired. Cover the use of temporary files for data exchange between parent and child processes and the role of pickle in serializing complex results. Include security considerations around code generation and execution. Provide examples of generated scripts for common analysis pipelines and troubleshooting tips for common execution failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Knowledge Base Extension
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for extending the RAG system's knowledge base with new tools or domains. Provide a step-by-step guide on formatting new tool documentation in TOOLS_REFERENCE.md style, registering tools in the system, and retraining the ChromaDB index. Explain how to customize embedding templates in metaknowledge_prompt.txt to accommodate new analysis types. Include best practices for structuring domain knowledge to maximize retrieval effectiveness. Demonstrate with an example how to add a new signal processing tool and ensure it's properly discoverable by the LLMOrchestrator. Address versioning concerns, backward compatibility, and validation procedures for extended knowledge. Offer debugging techniques for verifying successful integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Decomposition Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create in-depth documentation for matrix decomposition and component selection tools: NMF (Non-negative Matrix Factorization) and component selector. Explain how NMF is used to decompose vibration signals into additive, interpretable components representing different fault sources or operational modes. Document the decomposition process, convergence criteria, and initialization methods. For select_component, detail how components are evaluated and selected based on sparsity, periodicity, or diagnostic relevance. Include usage patterns within autonomous pipelines where LLMOrchestrator chooses components for further analysis. Provide examples of input/output data structures and integration with visualization modules. Address common challenges like over-decomposition, basis ambiguity, and computational load, with tuning recommendations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Result Evaluation Prompts
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the result evaluation prompt system. Explain how evaluate_local_prompt.txt and its v2 version enable the LLM to assess intermediate analysis results against user objectives and domain-specific criteria. Describe how these prompts integrate qualitative observations with quantitative metrics from the parameterization module, using variables like {result_visualization}, {metric_values}, and {expected_signature}. Provide examples of evaluation prompts that lead to decisions such as 'continue analysis', 'refine parameters', or 'terminate pipeline'. Detail the iterative feedback loop where LLM assessments inform the next action in the orchestration workflow. Discuss improvements in v2, including structured output formatting and clearer decision boundaries. Include best practices for tuning evaluation criteria based on application domain (e.g., bearing fault vs. gearbox analysis). Address common issues like overconfidence in assessments, metric misinterpretation, and contradictory feedback. Explain how prompt design influences convergence speed and analysis reliability.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tool Path and Execution Settings
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for tool path and execution settings in mcpsettings.json. Explain how the system locates and loads modular tools from src/tools/, including directory search paths and dynamic import mechanisms. Document configuration options for tool execution, such as subprocess timeouts, concurrency limits, and logging verbosity. Show how to customize tool discovery for external or user-defined modules. Provide examples of configuring specialized toolchains for different signal processing workflows. Address troubleshooting steps for missing tools or import failures, including path resolution and permission issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Industrial Vibration Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop a practical guide for general industrial vibration monitoring scenarios. Cover continuous and periodic monitoring use cases across various equipment types (pumps, motors, compressors). Explain how the system uses baseline_measurement.py to establish normal operating profiles and detect deviations. Detail the autonomous pipeline generation for FFT-based spectral trending, including bandpass filtering to isolate frequency bands of interest. Showcase how the LLMOrchestrator adapts analysis strategies based on equipment type and operating conditions specified in the objective. Include examples of automated report generation, threshold-based alerting, and integration with maintenance workflows. Demonstrate the system's ability to combine multiple tools (filtering, FFT, statistical parameterization) into cohesive monitoring pipelines. Provide configuration templates for common machinery and discuss long-term data storage considerations. Highlight how quantitative_parameterization_module.py enables objective comparison across measurement sessions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lowpass Filter
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the lowpass_filter tool. Explain its application in eliminating high-frequency noise or aliasing components from vibration data, preserving only the lower frequency content relevant to machine health monitoring. Document the function signature, parameters (cutoff frequency, fs, order), return values, and implementation using scipy.signal.butter and scipy.signal.filtfilt. Include real-world examples from the codebase where it is used as a preprocessing step before envelope analysis or spectral decomposition. Highlight the use of forward-backward filtering to maintain phase integrity. Refer to lowpass_filter.md for best practice configurations. Discuss common issues such as passband ripple, phase delay (mitigated by filtfilt), and edge effects, with practical solutions. Provide optimization guidance for processing long-duration signals efficiently.

wiki_catalogs.name: Signal Spectrogram
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop in-depth documentation for the Signal Spectrogram tool, which visualizes time-varying frequency content using Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT). Explain its application in identifying non-stationary behaviors such as transient impacts, speed variations, or evolving faults. Document algorithmic implementation including window type (e.g., Hamming), overlap percentage, and FFT size. Describe output format as a 2D time-frequency matrix and its rendering in GUI heatmaps. Provide practical examples for monitoring startup/shutdown sequences or detecting intermittent faults. Discuss trade-offs between time and frequency resolution based on window settings. Include performance tips for handling long-duration signals and memory-efficient computation. Link to the markdown guide for recommended configurations in variable-speed machinery analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the core system components. Describe the orchestrator pattern centered around LLMOrchestrator as the decision-making hub. Explain how it integrates with ContextManager for state persistence, prompt_assembler for dynamic prompt generation, rag_builder for domain knowledge retrieval, and quantitative_parameterization_module for result evaluation. Detail the data flow between components, including metaknowledge construction, action sequence generation, and iterative refinement. Illustrate component interactions with sequence and component diagrams. Discuss design trade-offs such as stateful execution vs. reproducibility, and the benefits of using RAG for domain-specific reasoning. Address scalability, error handling, and failure recovery mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: RAG System Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the rag_builder component and its integration with the tool documentation system. Explain how it constructs a ChromaDB vector index from tool documentation files (.md) to enable semantic retrieval of domain knowledge during pipeline orchestration. Detail the ingestion pipeline that processes markdown files from src/tools/ into embeddings using LangChain. Describe the query interface used by LLMOrchestrator to retrieve relevant tool descriptions, usage examples, and technical specifications based on current analysis context. Illustrate the retrieval-augmented decision-making process where the LLM combines general reasoning with precise tool capabilities. Document the index building process, including text splitting strategies, embedding models, and similarity search parameters. Address performance considerations for index querying latency and memory usage. Provide guidance on extending the knowledge base with new tool documentation and maintaining consistency between code and documentation.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM-Driven Decision Making and Evaluation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the _evaluate_result and _fetch_next_action methods that form the core decision-making engine. Explain how the LLM evaluates intermediate results using multimodal context including metaknowledge, action history, and domain-specific RAG retrievals. Document the JSON schema used for evaluation responses (is_useful, is_final, justification, tool_name, params). Describe how parameter inheritance works through SequenceMatcher-based key matching with accept_ratio threshold. Illustrate the feedback loop where evaluation directly informs the next pipeline step. Include examples of effective vs. ineffective evaluations and strategies for improving LLM decision quality through prompt engineering. Address common failure modes such as oscillating decisions or premature termination.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the load_data utility tool responsible for importing and preprocessing heterogeneous data formats (e.g., MATLAB .mat files via h5py, CSV, binary). Describe supported file types, data structure assumptions, channel mapping, and timestamp handling. Explain integration with the GUI data loader and how metadata is extracted and passed to the metaknowledge system. Include code examples showing programmatic and orchestrated usage. Detail error handling for corrupted files, inconsistent sampling rates, or missing headers. Provide guidance on extending the loader for custom formats. Link to configuration options in mcpsettings.json that affect data loading behavior. Address performance considerations for large industrial datasets and memory management strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Prompt Assembly Mechanism
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the prompt_assembler.py module, which serves as the engine for dynamic prompt generation. Explain its role in loading templates, resolving variables, and injecting runtime context from the LLMOrchestrator and ContextManager. Describe key methods such as `assemble_metaknowledge_prompt()`, `assemble_pipeline_prompt()`, and `assemble_evaluation_prompt()`, including their parameters and return types. Provide code examples showing how template strings are combined with data from metaknowledge, tool registry, and result history. Discuss error handling for missing variables, template loading failures, and size limitations. Include performance considerations around string formatting efficiency and memory usage during high-frequency orchestration cycles. Document the configuration system for template paths and version selection (v1 vs v2). Address extension points for supporting new prompt types or multi-LLM workflows. Explain integration with the RAG system to inject retrieved knowledge snippets into prompts dynamically.

wiki_catalogs.name: Smoothing Parameter Customization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for smoothing parameter customization in rlowess_smoother.py. Explain the LOWESS (Locally Weighted Scatterplot Smoothing) algorithm implementation and its role in preprocessing noisy vibration signals. Document configurable parameters such as smoothing window size, polynomial degree, and robustness iterations. Provide practical examples showing the impact of different settings on signal clarity and artifact preservation. Include usage patterns from within analysis pipelines and integration with transform tools. Offer best practice guidelines for tuning parameters based on signal characteristics like sampling rate and noise level. Address performance implications of high-precision smoothing on large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: CSC Map
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the CSC (Cepstral Spectral Correlation) Map tool, used for detecting cyclostationary features in vibration signals indicative of repetitive impacts from damaged components. Explain the mathematical foundation involving spectral correlation density and cepstral transformation to highlight periodic modulations. Document the processing pipeline: segmentation, spectral correlation computation, and quefrency-domain visualization. Describe input requirements, parameter tuning (e.g., block size, frequency resolution), and interpretation of peaks in the cepstrum related to fault repetition rates. Provide use cases in gearbox and bearing diagnostics where traditional FFT may fail. Include implementation details leveraging scipy.signal and optimization techniques for computational efficiency. Reference the markdown guide for advanced configuration in low-SNR environments and multi-component signal separation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analysis Pipeline Orchestration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the analysis pipeline orchestration system. Explain how the LLMOrchestrator designs, executes, and iteratively refines data analysis pipelines based on user objectives and intermediate results. Cover the execution model: from metaknowledge extraction to tool selection, script generation, subprocess execution, and result assessment. Detail state persistence using pickle and context restoration across sessions. Document the decision loop involving quantitative parameterization and LLM evaluation. Include practical examples of pipeline evolution during fault detection tasks. Address common issues like infinite loops, tool execution failures, and suboptimal pipeline decisions, with debugging strategies and mitigation techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Quantitative Parameterization and Evaluation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the quantitative_parameterization_module. Explain its role in objectively assessing analysis results through domain-specific metrics for vibration analysis and signal processing. Document the calculation methods for key parameters such as crest factor, kurtosis, RMS, peak-to-peak amplitude, and frequency band energy distribution. Describe how these metrics are used by LLMOrchestrator to evaluate the effectiveness of executed pipelines and inform subsequent decisions. Detail the integration pattern where numerical results are transformed into qualitative assessments for LLM interpretation. Illustrate the feedback loop between tool execution, parameter calculation, and pipeline optimization. Provide examples of how specific parameter thresholds indicate successful fault detection or signal characteristics. Address accuracy considerations, numerical stability, and edge cases in metric computation. Include guidance on extending the module with custom evaluation metrics for specialized analysis scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Knowledge Integration (RAG System)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system. Explain how rag_builder.py constructs and queries a ChromaDB vector store from tool documentation and domain knowledge to inform LLM decisions. Describe the index building process, embedding strategies using LangChain, and context retrieval mechanisms. Detail how retrieved knowledge is integrated into metaknowledge construction and prompt assembly. Provide examples of how RAG prevents hallucinations and improves tool selection accuracy. Include setup instructions for extending the knowledge base with new tools or domains. Address performance considerations like indexing time and query latency, and troubleshooting tips for relevance issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tool Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive reference documentation for all analysis tools. For each tool, document its purpose, parameters, return values, and usage patterns. Group tools by category: signal processing (filters), transforms (FFT, spectrogram, envelope spectrum, CSC map), decomposition (NMF, component selection), and utilities (data loading). Include code examples showing how each tool is invoked within analysis pipelines. Explain the mathematical foundations where relevant (e.g., FFT, NMF). Link each tool to its corresponding markdown documentation and source file. Address common usage errors and performance tips. Provide guidance on selecting appropriate tools for specific analysis objectives.

wiki_catalogs.name: Prompt Engineering
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the prompt engineering system. Explain how prompt_assembler.py dynamically constructs context-aware prompts using templates from the prompt_templates directory. Describe the structure and purpose of key templates: metaknowledge_prompt for context extraction, meta_template_prompt for pipeline design, and evaluate_local_prompt for result assessment. Detail how variables like data characteristics, user objectives, and intermediate results are injected into templates. Provide examples of rendered prompts at different pipeline stages. Include best practices for modifying or extending templates. Address common issues like prompt overflow, ambiguous instructions, and template versioning. Discuss how prompt design influences LLM behavior and analysis quality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Quantitative Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the quantitative parameterization system. Explain how quantitative_parameterization_module.py calculates domain-specific metrics (e.g., kurtosis, crest factor, RMS) to evaluate analysis results and guide pipeline optimization. Detail the statistical methods used and their relevance to vibration analysis. Describe how baseline measurements from baseline_measurement.py and baseline_results.json are used for comparison and anomaly detection. Provide examples of metric interpretation in fault diagnosis scenarios. Include guidance on extending the module with new metrics. Address accuracy considerations, numerical stability, and performance optimization for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for all public interfaces in the codebase. For each core module (LLMOrchestrator, ContextManager, prompt_assembler, rag_builder), document classes, methods, parameters, return types, and exceptions. Use examples from actual code to illustrate usage patterns. Specify initialization requirements, state dependencies, and thread safety considerations. Include event hooks and callback mechanisms where applicable. Document the tool registry API and how external tools are registered and invoked. Address versioning, deprecation policies, and backward compatibility. Provide client implementation examples for integrating the core engine into other applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Advanced Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for advanced configuration options. Explain the structure and purpose of mcpsettings.json, including API keys, model parameters, tool paths, and execution settings. Document authentication mechanisms in authentication.py for securing LLM access. Cover customization options such as modifying smoothing parameters in rlowess_smoother.py or extending tool search paths. Provide examples of configuration for different analysis scenarios (e.g., high-frequency vibration, low-SNR signals). Include security best practices for handling credentials. Address environment-specific configuration and troubleshooting configuration-related failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create a guide for developers contributing to the project. Explain the overall code structure, module responsibilities, and import hierarchy. Provide contribution guidelines covering coding standards, testing practices, and pull request workflows. Detail the process for adding new analysis tools, including interface requirements and registration with the orchestrator. Include debugging strategies using logging and state inspection. Document testing approaches for both GUI and core logic. Provide tips for extending the system with new features like additional transform types or visualization modes. Address common development pitfalls and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Use Cases and Examples
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content showcasing practical use cases and examples. Include step-by-step walkthroughs of common scenarios: bearing fault detection using envelope spectrum analysis, gearbox fault identification with NMF decomposition, and general industrial vibration monitoring. For each use case, describe the problem, data characteristics, analysis pipeline generated, and interpretation of results. Use test_run_button.py and baseline_measurement.py as references for test scenarios. Include code snippets and configuration settings used in each example. Provide performance benchmarks and accuracy metrics where available. Highlight how the autonomous system adapts its approach based on data characteristics and objectives.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>
# Comprehensive Project Analysis: AIDA (AI-Driven Analyzer)

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
AIDA (AI-Driven Analyzer) is an autonomous AI-driven data analysis system that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to design and execute complex data processing pipelines without human intervention. The system eliminates manual tool selection and parameter tuning in data analysis workflows by using Google's Gemini LLM to understand user objectives, plan optimal analysis pipelines, execute them autonomously, evaluate results, and iteratively refine the approach.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Autonomous Analysis**: Enable zero manual configuration by allowing users to describe analysis goals in natural language
- **Intelligent Tool Selection**: Use LLMs to choose optimal tools based on data characteristics and analysis objectives
- **Adaptive Parameter Tuning**: Automatically refine parameters based on intermediate results
- **Self-Correction**: Implement automatic pipeline modification when steps prove unproductive
- **Complete Script Generation**: Generate both analysis results and the complete, executable Python script

### Target Audience
- **Industrial Engineers**: For vibration analysis and bearing fault detection in industrial applications
- **Data Scientists**: For automated signal processing and pattern discovery in scientific research
- **Quality Control Specialists**: For product quality assessment through vibration signatures and process monitoring
- **Researchers**: For automated feature extraction and classification in signal analysis

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
AIDA follows a modular architecture with clear separation of concerns, consisting of several core components that work together to enable autonomous data analysis.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Interface] --> B[LLM Orchestrator]
    B --> C[RAG System]
    B --> D[Tool Registry]
    B --> E[Prompt Assembler]
    D --> F[Signal Processing Tools]
    D --> G[Transform Tools]
    D --> H[Decomposition Tools]
    D --> I[Utility Tools]
    B --> J[Quantitative Parameterization]
    B --> K[Context Manager]
    C --> L[Vector Store]
    E --> M[Prompt Templates]
    B --> N[Script Execution]
    N --> O[Subprocess]
```

### Design Patterns
The system implements several key design patterns:

- **Orchestrator Pattern**: The `LLMOrchestrator` serves as the central decision-making engine that manages the entire analysis workflow
- **Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)**: Combines LLM capabilities with domain-specific knowledge retrieval for context-aware decision making
- **Plugin Architecture**: Modular tool system allows for easy extension with new analysis capabilities
- **State Management**: Persistent execution state across pipeline steps using pickle-based serialization
- **Multimodal Intelligence**: Leverages both visual and quantitative analysis for comprehensive result evaluation

### System Relationships
The components interact through well-defined interfaces, with the LLM Orchestrator serving as the central hub that coordinates all activities. The system follows a request-response pattern where the orchestrator generates prompts, sends them to the LLM, processes the responses, and executes the resulting actions.

```mermaid
graph TD
    App[GUI Application] -->|User Input| Orchestrator[LLM Orchestrator]
    Orchestrator -->|Prompt| LLM[Google Gemini API]
    LLM -->|Response| Orchestrator
    Orchestrator -->|Action Sequence| ScriptGenerator[Script Generator]
    ScriptGenerator -->|Python Script| Subprocess[Subprocess Execution]
    Subprocess -->|Results| Orchestrator
    Orchestrator -->|Evaluation| LLM
    RAG[RAG System] -->|Context| Orchestrator
    Tools[Tool Registry] -->|Tool Documentation| Orchestrator
    PromptAssembler[Prompt Assembler] -->|Structured Prompts| Orchestrator
    Quantitative[Quantitative Parameterization] -->|Metrics| Orchestrator
```

### Data Flow Architecture
The data flows through the system in a structured pipeline, starting from user input and progressing through analysis steps to final results.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    UserInput[User Input] --> Metaknowledge[Metaknowledge Construction]
    DataFiles[Data Files] --> Metaknowledge
    Metaknowledge --> InitialPipeline[Initial Pipeline]
    InitialPipeline --> IterativeExecution[Iterative Execution]
    IterativeExecution --> ScriptGeneration[Script Generation]
    ScriptGeneration --> SubprocessExecution[Subprocess Execution]
    SubprocessExecution --> ResultEvaluation[Result Evaluation]
    ResultEvaluation --> DecisionMaking[Decision Making]
    DecisionMaking --> PipelineCompletion[Pipeline Completion]
    PipelineCompletion --> FinalOutput[Final Output]
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- [app.py](src/app.py): Application entry point that initializes the GUI and starts the analysis process

### Core Modules
- [LLMOrchestrator.py](src/core/LLMOrchestrator.py): Central decision-making engine that manages the entire analysis workflow
- [rag_builder.py](src/core/rag_builder.py): RAG system that provides domain-specific knowledge during decision making
- [prompt_assembler.py](src/core/prompt_assembler.py): Constructs context-aware prompts for different LLM interactions
- [quantitative_parameterization_module.py](src/core/quantitative_parameterization_module.py): Calculates domain-specific metrics for result evaluation
- [ContextManager.py](src/core/ContextManager.py): Manages conversation context and history

### Configuration Approach
- [mcpsettings.json](mcpsettings.json): Configuration file for system settings and API credentials
- Environment variables for sensitive information like API keys
- Pre-built vector stores for fast retrieval of domain knowledge
- Modular tool system with standardized interfaces

### External Dependencies
- [requirements.txt](requirements.txt): Python dependencies including Google Gemini API, LangChain, ChromaDB, and various scientific computing libraries
- Google Gemini API for LLM capabilities
- ChromaDB for vector storage
- HuggingFace all-MiniLM-L12-v2 for embeddings

### Integration Points
- Google Gemini API for multimodal AI capabilities
- LangChain for RAG implementation
- ChromaDB for vector storage
- Various Python scientific computing libraries (numpy, scipy, matplotlib)

### Component Relationships
```mermaid
graph TD
    GUI[GUI Interface] --> Orchestrator[LLM Orchestrator]
    Orchestrator --> LLM[Google Gemini]
    Orchestrator --> RAG[RAG System]
    Orchestrator --> Tools[Tool Registry]
    Orchestrator --> Prompt[Prompt Assembler]
    Orchestrator --> Quantitative[Quantitative Parameterization]
    RAG --> VectorStore[Vector Store]
    Prompt --> Templates[Prompt Templates]
    Tools --> SigProc[Signal Processing]
    Tools --> Transforms[Transforms]
    Tools --> Decomposition[Decomposition]
    Tools --> Utils[Utilities]
    Quantitative --> Metrics[Statistical Metrics]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
AIDA provides a comprehensive set of features for autonomous data analysis:

- **Autonomous Analysis**: LLM-driven pipeline orchestration with minimal human intervention
- **Multimodal Intelligence**: Visual analysis of results using Google Gemini's multimodal capabilities
- **RAG-Enhanced**: Retrieval-Augmented Generation with domain-specific knowledge base
- **Modular Tools**: Extensible toolkit for signal processing, transforms, and decomposition
- **Interactive GUI**: User-friendly interface built with CustomTkinter
- **Real-time Feedback**: Visual and quantitative evaluation of analysis results

### Implementation Highlights
- **Autonomous Pipeline Execution**: The system can autonomously design and execute complete analysis pipelines based on natural language descriptions
- **Self-Correction Mechanisms**: Automatic pipeline modification when steps prove unproductive
- **State Persistence**: Pickle-based data serialization between steps ensures robust state management
- **Multimodal Evaluation**: Combines visual analysis with quantitative metrics for comprehensive result assessment
- **Dynamic Script Generation**: Converts action sequences to executable Python scripts in real-time

### Feature Architecture
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> DataLoading: Load Data
    DataLoading --> Metaknowledge: Construct Metaknowledge
    Metaknowledge --> InitialPlanning: Generate Initial Plan
    InitialPlanning --> ActionExecution: Execute Action
    ActionExecution --> ResultEvaluation: Evaluate Results
    ResultEvaluation --> DecisionMaking: Make Decision
    DecisionMaking --> ActionExecution: Continue Pipeline
    DecisionMaking --> PipelineCompletion: Complete Pipeline
    PipelineCompletion --> ScriptGeneration: Generate Script
    ScriptGeneration --> FinalOutput: Present Results
    FinalOutput --> Idle: Reset
```

### Signal Processing Capabilities
AIDA includes a comprehensive set of signal processing tools specifically designed for vibration analysis and industrial applications:

```mermaid
graph TD
    SignalProcessing[Signal Processing]
    SignalProcessing --> Lowpass[Lowpass Filter]
    SignalProcessing --> Highpass[Highpass Filter]
    SignalProcessing --> Bandpass[Bandpass Filter]
    
    Transforms[Transforms]
    Transforms --> FFT[FFT Spectrum]
    Transforms --> Envelope[Envelope Spectrum]
    Transforms --> Spectrogram[Spectrogram]
    Transforms --> CSC[CSC Map]
    
    Decomposition[Decomposition]
    Decomposition --> NMF[NMF Decomposition]
    Decomposition --> Component[Component Selection]
    
    Utilities[Utilities]
    Utilities --> LoadData[Load Data]
```

### Knowledge Integration
The RAG system provides domain-specific knowledge during the analysis process, enhancing the LLM's decision-making capabilities:

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    KnowledgeBase[Knowledge Base] -->|Scientific Papers| RAG[RAG System]
    ToolDocs[Tool Documentation] -->|Documentation| RAG
    DomainRules[Domain Rules] -->|Expertise| RAG
    RAG -->|Context| LLM[LLM Orchestrator]
    LLM -->|Informed Decisions| Analysis[Analysis Pipeline]
```
</blog>
